input.query: Where does FilterRunners skip or filter runners by mode?

content.text:
Identifying the issue: `ops.RebuildImage` rejects the whole operation if any filtered runner is native (`internal/ops/ops.go` lines 192–196). `runner.Manager.RebuildImage` also returns an error for native mode. We'll plan filtering + no-op behavior and doc/help updates.

[REDACTED]

input.name: Rebuild skip native
input.overview: Change `gh sr rebuild` so it rebuilds only `runner_mode: container` runners by filtering the target list, skips native rows without failing, and optionally tightens the manager API to a no-op for native instead of an error.
input.plan:
# Plan: `gh sr rebuild` skips native runners

## Current behavior

- [`internal/ops/ops.go`](internal/ops/ops.go) — After `config.FilterRunners`, `RebuildImage` loops all matched runners and **returns an error** if any `!rc.IsContainerMode()` (native / default mode).
- [`internal/runner/runner.go`](internal/runner/runner.go) — `(*Manager).RebuildImage` also **errors** on native as a second guard.

So a mixed selection, or `rebuild` with no args when the config includes native runners, fails fast even though container runners could be rebuilt.

## Desired behavior

- **Act only on container-mode runners**: pass only `rc.IsContainerMode()` rows into `runPerHostParallel` (same parallel-per-host behavior as today).
- **Do not error on native**: native rows in the filtered set are **skipped** with a short note on `w` (stderr in normal CLI use is `OutOrStdout` from cobra — same as today’s errors).

## Implementation

### 1. `ops.RebuildImage` ([`internal/ops/ops.go`](internal/ops/ops.go))

- After `runners := config.FilterRunners(...)`, partition into `containerRunners` vs skipped natives (single pass).
- For each skipped native: `fmt.Fprintf(w, ...)` explaining that rebuild is container-only (mirror the old error text tone, but as info, not `return fmt.Errorf`).
- If `len(containerRunners) == 0`: print a single line such as **no container-mode runners to rebuild** (so the command does not exit silently) and `return nil` **without** calling `runPerHostParallel` (empty slice already no-ops, but explicit message is clearer).
- Call `runPerHostParallel(w, cfg, containerRunners, ...)` unchanged for the rebuild body.
- Update the function comment: native runners are **skipped**, not rejected.

### 2. `runner.Manager.RebuildImage` ([`internal/runner/runner.go`](internal/runner/runner.go))

- Change the native branch from `return fmt.Errorf(...)` to **`return nil`** (optionally `fmt.Fprintf(m.out(), ...)` once for consistency if this path is ever hit). This keeps the public API safe if another call site appears later.

### 3. CLI help ([`cmd/gh-sr/main.go`](cmd/gh-sr/main.go))

- Adjust `rebuildCmd` `Long` text: replace “Only container-mode runners are supported” with language that **native runners are skipped** and only container-mode rows are rebuilt.

### 4. Docs (optional but small)

- [`docs/content/guides/agentic-workflows.md`](docs/content/guides/agentic-workflows.md) — In “Rebuild the runner image”, add one sentence that native-mode rows in the selection are skipped (no error).

## Tests (recommended, small)

- Add [`internal/ops/rebuild_test.go`](internal/ops/rebuild_test.go) (or extend an existing ops test file) with a **table-driven test** of a tiny unexported helper, e.g. `partitionRebuildTargets(runners []config.RunnerConfig) (container, skipped []config.RunnerConfig)`, so behavior is locked without SSH/mocks. If you prefer zero new symbols, assert behavior indirectly only where feasible; the helper keeps the test trivial.

## Flow (after change)

```mermaid
flowchart LR
  filter[FilterRunners]
  part[Keep container only]
  skip[Log skip for native]
  run[runPerHostParallel rebuild]
  filter --> part
  part --> skip
  part --> run
```

No config schema changes; `IsContainerMode()` in [`internal/config/config.go`](internal/config/config.go) remains the source of truth (`runner_mode == "container"`).

todos.content: Partition filtered runners in ops.RebuildImage; skip native with messages; early exit when no container targets
Change runner.Manager.RebuildImage native branch to nil (optional out log)
Update rebuildCmd Long + agentic-workflows.md rebuild blurb
Add small ops test for partition/filter logic (helper or direct slice test)

Native-mode runners in the selection are skipped without error; the command only rebuilds `runner_mode: container` runners.

gh sr rebuild <runner-name>   # preferred: tears down containers, rebuilds, restarts

input.description: Run Go tests for ops, runner, cmd packages

todos.content: Update rebuildCmd Long + agentic-workflows.md rebuild blurb
Add small ops test for partition/filter logic (helper or direct slice test)

content.text:
Here is what was implemented.

### Behavior

- **[`internal/ops/ops.go`](internal/ops/ops.go)** — Added `partitionRebuildTargets`. `RebuildImage` now logs each native runner as skipped, rebuilds only `runner_mode: container` runners via `runPerHostParallel`, and if none remain prints `No container-mode runners to rebuild.` and exits **0**.
- **[`internal/runner/runner.go`](internal/runner/runner.go)** — `RebuildImage` on native is a **no-op** (`nil`) with a one-line message on `m.out()` if it is ever called without going through `ops` first.
- **[`cmd/gh-sr/main.go`](cmd/gh-sr/main.go)** — `rebuild` long help updated to say native rows are skipped.
- **[`docs/content/guides/agentic-workflows.md`](docs/content/guides/agentic-workflows.md)** — One sentence added in the rebuild section.
- **[`internal/ops/rebuild_test.go`](internal/ops/rebuild_test.go)** — Table tests for `partitionRebuildTargets`.

`go test ./...` passes. All related todos are marked completed.
